response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and systems engineer with a broad portfolio, but his background is heavily centered on TypeScript/Node.js web development with only peripheral C++ exposure — a brief stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project. The role demands deep, production-grade modern C++ (or Rust) expertise for high-performance geospatial pipeline work, which is not present. While he has genuine GIS/geospatial interest through GISCollective and Green Map, these are hobbyist/open-source projects using web technologies rather than native C++ routing tile pipelines. His distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby is the strongest adjacent signal, but it does not bridge the gap to the required navigation systems domain.
strengths: Genuine, sustained engagement with geospatial data through GISCollective and Green Map projects, demonstrating domain interest and map data familiarity
Distributed systems experience handling millions of requests/second at Dunnhumby using high-performance async patterns — relevant architectural mindset
Broad systems breadth including Linux, embedded work, and a history of picking the right tool for the job across multiple paradigms
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and dated (2014–2015); no modern C++ or Rust production work visible anywhere in the resume
No experience with routing tiles, navigation pipelines, or real-time traffic data integration — the core technical domain of the role
Career trajectory is firmly in web/full-stack (TypeScript, Node.js, React) with no recent movement toward systems or native-code engineering
